verses.text: The LORD: command Israel to give the Levites cities to dwell in, with suburbs round about for their cattle. Measure from the wall outward a thousand cubits, then two thousand cubits east, south, west, north — the city in the midst. These are the suburbs. Guidance: The Levites have no tribal territory — but they get cities scattered through every tribe. Their inheritance is the LORD; their address is everywhere. God's ministers are distributed, not concentrated, so worship is close to every doorstep.

Six cities for refuge that the slayer may flee unto, and forty-two cities besides — forty-eight cities for the Levites, with their suburbs. Of the tribes that have many, give more; of the tribes that have few, give fewer. Guidance: Six refuges and forty-two homes — every Levite serving and every fugitive safe. The big tribes give more cities, the small ones give fewer. God writes proportional generosity into the law: more from those who have more, no shame for those who have less.

The LORD: when ye pass over Jordan, appoint cities of refuge — that the manslayer who killeth any unawares may flee thither. Six cities — three on this side Jordan, three in Canaan — for Israel, stranger, and sojourner. Guidance: A place to run before the avenger arrives. The verb is appointed — God plans for the awful moment when blood gets spilled by accident. And the door of refuge opens equally to the citizen and the foreigner. Mercy in the law is not picky about passports.

If he smote with iron, with stone, with wood instrument — and the man die — he is a murderer; the murderer shall surely be put to death. Hate, lying in wait, smiting from enmity — murderer; the avenger of blood shall slay him when he meeteth him. Guidance: The text names the weapons and the motives. Iron, stone, wood — none of them softens the verdict. Hatred, ambush, enmity — the heart's posture decides. God's justice is not blind to intent; murder is what it is, and the avenger's role is real.

If he thrust him suddenly without enmity, or cast something without laying in wait, and the man die — the congregation shall judge between the slayer and the avenger. The congregation shall deliver the slayer from the avenger, restore him to the city of refuge; he shall abide there until the death of the high priest. Guidance: A trial, not a lynching. The congregation hears both sides — the family wanting blood and the manslayer pleading accident. God puts a judging community between grief and revenge. And His refuge is real — the high priest's death becomes the bond that frees the unlucky.

If the slayer come without the border of his city of refuge, and the avenger find him, slay him — no blood guilt. He should have abode in his city of refuge until the death of the high priest; after the priest's death, the manslayer may return. Guidance: Refuge is real only while you stay inside it. Step out, and the safety ends. The death of the high priest opens the gate — a foreshadow of another High Priest whose death would free every fugitive forever, no matter where they wandered.

Whoso killeth shall be put to death by mouth of witnesses; one witness shall not suffice. No ransom for the life of a murderer. So shall ye not pollute the land — blood defileth, and the land cannot be cleansed but by the blood of him that shed it. For I the LORD dwell among the children of Israel. Guidance: Two witnesses or no execution — God's law refuses to let one accuser end a life. No money buys a murderer out of judgment. And the reason is the deepest in the Torah: I dwell among them. The land is sacred because He is there.
